zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie於1337年之前出生在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾圖時，她的父親John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie19歲，母親Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne20歲。她於1351年5月3日之前與William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾登諾塔結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1410年在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾斯同哈文，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie于1337年之前出生在英国苏格兰斯林史爾圖时，她的父亲John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie19岁，母亲Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne20岁。她于1351年5月3日之前与William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine在英国苏格兰金加迪爾登諾塔结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1410年在英国苏格兰金加迪爾斯同哈文，埋葬在英国苏格兰金加迪爾。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frayser

variants.name: Frasier

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Frashier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie was born before 1337, in Touch, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie, was 19 and her mother, Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, was 20. She married William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine before 3 May 1351, in Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1410, in Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, and was buried in Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie est née avant 1337, à Touch, Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine avant 3 mai 1351, à Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1410, à Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie vor 1337 in Touch, Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie 19 und ihre Mutter Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne 20. Sie hat vor 3. Mai 1351 in Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1410 in Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie è nata il prima del 1337 nel Touch, Stirlingshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine prima del 3 maggio 1351, nel Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1410, nel Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowieが1337年以前にイギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイアトウチで生まれた時，父John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowieは19歳，母Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyneは20歳であった。1351年5月3日以前にイギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアダノッタルでWilliam Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1410年にイギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアストーネヘイベンで死去し，イギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie(이)가 1337년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 토우치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1351년 5월 3일 이전 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르 던타르에서 William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르 스톤하븐에서 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie nasceu antes 1337, em Touch, Stirlingshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine antes 3 de maio de 1351, em Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1410, em Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie родилась перед 1337 г. в местности тоуч, стирлингшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine перед 3 май 1351 г. в местности дунноттар, кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1410 г. в местности стоунэавен, кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Fraser of Touchfraser and Cowie nació antes de 1337, en Touch, Stirlingshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, John Fraser Of Touchfraser And Cowie, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Mary Bisset, Heiress Of The Lands Of Aboyne, tenía 20 años. Se casó con William Keith of Galston, 2nd Lord Marischal, Sheriff of Kincardine antes de 3 de mayo de 1351, en Dunnottar, Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1410, en Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
